mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.25% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 5.0% depresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.7% signals a tight labor market near full employment. When UNRATE remains below 5.0% for consecutive months, wage pressures and inflation expectations rise, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 5.0% for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns reverses sign over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1795k units indicate robust residential construction activity. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1600k over 2 months signals weakening housing demand, which precedes broader economic slowdown and equity market contraction within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines below 1600k for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: housing cycle peak or early contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.7 reflects current manufacturing capacity utilization. A 3-month moving average decline in INDPRO signals deteriorating supply-side momentum and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month moving average declines by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 368.2B and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135.5M jointly indicate consumer spending capacity. When RSAFS growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while PAYEMS growth simultaneously slows below 0.1% month-over-month, aggregate demand weakens, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0.2% AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.1% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368233.0M and PAYEMS at 135500.0k as of 2006-08-15
regime_hint: demand-side deceleration with synchronized labor and consumption weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured as the coefficient on RSAFS*PAYEMS interaction term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 203.7 combined with FEDFUNDS at 5.25% creates a real interest rate environment. When the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.5% while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, real rates are restrictive and rising, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 5-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-08-15
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11397.6B represents the demand foundation. A quarterly growth rate below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration. When GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% in the most recent quarter, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days are negative as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135.5M and unemployment (UNRATE) at 4.7% jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 0.05% while UNRATE simultaneously rises above 4.8%, labor market deterioration signals recession risk, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.05% AND UNRATE > 4.8% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135500.0k and UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2006-08-15
regime_hint: labor market inflection point with employment deceleration and rising joblessness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect between employment growth and unemployment is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
